channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Icons to perform at John Farnham tribute concert
item.description: Music stars including Celine Dion, Hugh Jackman and Keith Urban will take the stage to honour Australian legend John Farnham at a tribute concert later this year.

media:group.media:description: A tribute concert will be held for John Farnham in Melbourne on September 20.

item.title: Tensions flare as police enter protest camp at Olympic stadium site
item.description: Tensions have flared at Victoria Park after police, Brisbane City Council officers and firefighters entered one of the protest camps ahead of the planned $3.6 billion Olympic stadium development.
item.dc:creator: Lottie Twyford and Shari Hams

media:group.media:description: Protesters have been told by police and council staff they need to pack up their tents.

item.title: Refugee who tried to flee the Taliban 15 times finally safe to play cricket
item.description: After the Taliban banned women's sport in Afghanistan, most of the women's cricket team ended up relocating to Australia. Now, they're on tour in the Northern Territory — "a happy ending" after a traumatic journey.
item.dc:creator: Tilda Colling

media:group.media:description: Firooza Afghan and her family are now refugees in Australia.

item.title: 'Everyone thought Sinner would win': French Open upset stuns tennis world
item.description: it seemed a foregone conclusion Jannik Sinner would win the French Open, which is why his meltdown amid the Paris heat wave was so unexpected.

media:group.media:description: Jannik Sinner struggled in the heat during his second-round loss to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo.

item.title: Second NSW government minister suspended over secret documents
item.description: Government frontbenchers John Graham and Penny Sharpe have been suspended from the upper house over failing to produce documents demanded by the opposition.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Lewis

media:group.media:description: John Graham and Penny Sharpe have both been suspended from parliament over the government's failure to produce documents.

item.title: Philosopher tasked with keeping Australians safe from AI
item.description: A philosopher and Royal Australian Air Force reservist has been chosen to lead the federal government's efforts to keep Australians safe from the harms of AI.
item.dc:creator: Cam Wilson

media:group.media:description: Kate Conroy has been described as a "global expert in responsible AI".

item.title: Last working steam tram of its kind gets two new drivers
item.description: Steam-powered trams ran in Rockhampton for 30 years until 1939 and are now displayed at a volunteer museum, between their Sunday and school holiday trips.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Jarrett

media:group.media:description: Michael Feldman-Budarick, 28, is one of the newest tram drivers at the Archer Park Rail Museum.

item.title: Cancer survivor's disappointment over hospital funding promise
item.description: A cancer survivor who stood alongside the Tasmanian premier at a 2025 election promise announcement says he is disappointed that the hospitals investment appears to be "on the back burner."
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Heard and Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff (front) and cancer patient Grant Howe (right) at the announcement in July 2025.

item.title: 'We're boxed in': Comedian Will Gibb on the perils of TikTok fame
item.description: As a comedian with legions of fans on social media, Will Gibb wishes he didn't still feel the need to legitimise himself. But he does.
item.dc:creator: Yasmin Jeffery

media:group.media:description: As a comedian with legions of fans on social media, Will Gibb wishes he didn't still feel the need to legitimise himself. But he does.

item.title: Australian star's injury hell continues as Giro comes too soon
item.description: Sarah Gigante, who finished third at last year's Giro d'Italia Women, will not take to the start line on Saturday after succumbing to a leg injury, continuing her horror run of injury.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Sarah Gigante is one of the best climbers in the women's peloton.

item.title: Push to vaccinate the vulnerable as WA diphtheria outbreak spreads
item.description: Health authorities in Western Australia's far north say they are battling disinformation and misconceptions amid a vaccination drive to contain a diphtheria outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Giulia Bertoglio

media:group.media:description: Vaccinations effort are ramping up in the East Kimberley to fight the diphtheria outbreak.

item.title: Former Victorian premier's new fence angers neighbours
item.description: A regional Victorian council says it is investigating complaints about 500 metres of fencing that was recently built along former premier John Brumby's property.
item.dc:creator: Nate Woodall

media:group.media:description: Locals say John Brumby's new fence is a danger to native animals.

item.title: Amazon sued over alleged button battery safety failure in kids backpacks
item.description: The consumer watchdog is suing Amazon alleging it sold children's unicorn backpacks on its online marketplace which failed to comply with mandatory button battery safety warnings.
item.dc:creator: Michael Atkin

media:group.media:description: The Unicorn Toddler Backpacks were designed for children and included a detachable light-up unicorn plush toy containing button batteries.

item.title: NSW skipper Kelly 'speechless' after Blues make State of Origin history
item.description: NSW captain Isabelle Kelly praises her Blues teammates after they complete a historic 3-0 State of Origin whitewash against Queensland.

media:group.media:description: Isabelle Kelly's side is the first NSW team to sweep an Origin series in 26 years.

item.title: Why $150m from Andrew Forrest's company may not be enough for this Pilbara community
item.description: More than a fortnight after winning the largest native title compensation payout ever in Australia, Pilbara leader Michael Woodley returns to his country.
item.dc:creator: Alistair Bates

media:group.media:description: Michael Woodley greets the river flowing near his home.

item.title: Our critics can't stop listening to these 10 albums from May
item.description: From long-awaited comebacks to First Nations glam rock and genre-defying polymaths, May was stacked with excellent new releases.
item.dc:creator: Al Newstead

media:group.media:description: Mulga Bore Hard Rock, Genesis Owusu, Ecca Vandal and Kneecap were among May's best album releases.

item.title: Live: KPMG boss resigns over mishandled whistleblower claims, ASX climbs
item.description: The CEO and head of audit of one of Australia's major accounting and consultancy firms resign over mishandled whistleblower allegations. On markets, the ASX is up solidly as oil prices drop on hopes of a Middle East peace deal. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Emilia Terzon

media:group.media:description: KPMG Australia's CEO has resigned.

item.title: Loopholes in laws make crackdown on illegal vapes difficult
item.description: More than 20 million illegal vapes, with a street value of just over $1 billion, have been seized by law enforcement authorities since Australia's vaping reforms came into effect.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Carbonell and Caitlyn Gribbin

media:group.media:description: More than 19 million vaping products have been seized by Australian Border Force (ABF) since January 2024.

item.title: Controversial Sunshine Coast housing development gets federal approval
item.description: Up to 12,000 homes could be added to the fast-growing region after a major hurdle was cleared for the contentious Aura South development to proceed.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Ross, Madeline Grace, Alex Easton, and Sarah Howells

media:group.media:description: The Aura South development would be built alongside the existing Aura and Pelican Waters communities.

item.title: Australia's Kasatkina sets up French Open clash with top seed Sabalenka
item.description: Daria Kasatkina will face world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the third round as fellow Australian Kimberly Birrell departs the singles draw in Paris.

media:group.media:description: Daria Kasatkina will meet Aryna Sabalenka in the third round.

item.title: This herb could halt a major NT gas project
item.description: The NT government is arguing its planned mega Middle Arm gas industry precinct will be needed to help shore up Australia's future fuel and energy supplies — but it has just identified that a tiny endangered herb could be growing in the way.
item.dc:creator: Jane Bardon

media:group.media:description: An environmental impact report for the Middle Arm project says it could threaten several endangered species, including this small herb.

item.title: Live: AG defends immunity claim for counterterrorism documents
item.description: The Attorney-General has defended the government's decision to block the antisemitism royal commission from accessing counterterrorism funding documents. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Communications Minister Michelle Rowland announced a promise to upgrade the NBN, 13 January 2025.

item.title: Kids protest against McDonald's after fast-food giant buys land in small town
item.description: Children turn out in protest after it's revealed the global fast-food giant has paid $4.6 million for a prominent site in central Victoria's Castlemaine.
item.dc:creator: Nate Woodall, Anna McGuinness, and Tyrone Dalton

media:group.media:description: Siblings Alby, 8, and 11-year-old Wren say they don't want a McDonald's in Castlemaine.

item.title: Canberra man jailed over sexual abuse of 'extremely vulnerable' child
item.description: A man who sexually assaulted a 12-year-old girl after a "chance encounter" at a Canberra shopping centre has been jailed, with the judge finding he took advantage of an "extremely vulnerable" child.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: Laurence Martin Coghlan was sentenced to four years and two months' jail.

item.title: 'Stunning' weekend for south-east Queensland before cool change
item.description: Queensland residents can expect a reprieve from the recent "wet and stormy" weather this weekend, with cooler-than-average temperatures on the way.

media:group.media:description: A rainbow appears on the Gold Coast.
